name: wcag-2.1-aa
description: WCAG 2.1 Level A and AA success-criterion rules. Layer 2 of the four-layer rubric composition per ADR-079.

severity_rules.pattern: Image (`` or background image conveying meaning) lacks descriptive `alt` text or aria-label.
severity_rules.description: WCAG 2.1 SC 1.1.1 (Level A) — non-text content must have a text alternative serving the equivalent purpose.
severity_rules.remediation: Add a meaningful `alt` attribute (or `alt=""` for decorative images); provide aria-label for icon-only controls.

severity_rules.pattern: Text foreground/background colour pair has contrast ratio below 4.5:1 for normal text or 3:1 for large text (24px+ or 18.66px+ bold).
severity_rules.description: WCAG 2.1 SC 1.4.3 (AA) — visual presentation of text must have sufficient contrast.
severity_rules.remediation: Increase contrast to >= 4.5:1 (>= 3:1 for large text); verify with an automated contrast checker.

severity_rules.pattern: Pre-recorded video or audio content lacks captions or a transcript.
severity_rules.description: WCAG 2.1 SC 1.2.2 (A) requires captions for prerecorded audio in synchronised media.
severity_rules.remediation: Provide captions track (WebVTT) for video and an HTML transcript for audio-only.

severity_rules.pattern: Interactive control bound to mouse-only events (`onmouseover`, `onmousedown`) without an equivalent keyboard handler.
severity_rules.description: WCAG 2.1 SC 2.1.1 (A) — all functionality must be operable through a keyboard interface.
severity_rules.remediation: Bind keyboard handlers (`onkeydown`/`onclick`); use semantic `<button>`/`<a>` elements for activations.

severity_rules.pattern: Page lacks a visible focus indicator (`outline: none` without a replacement) or has tab-order traps.
severity_rules.description: WCAG 2.1 SC 2.4.7 (AA) — the keyboard focus indicator must be visible.
severity_rules.remediation: Restore a high-contrast focus ring; ensure tab order matches visual reading order; avoid `tabindex > 0`.

severity_rules.pattern: Animation or content flashes more than three times per second within a one-second window.
severity_rules.description: WCAG 2.1 SC 2.3.1 (A) prohibits content that flashes more than three times per second to prevent seizures.
severity_rules.remediation: Reduce flash frequency below 3 Hz or limit the area to under the safe threshold; honour `prefers-reduced-motion`.

severity_rules.pattern: Form input that fails validation has no programmatically-associated error message (no `aria-describedby` or label-for-error link).
severity_rules.description: WCAG 2.1 SC 3.3.1 (A) — input errors must be identified and described to the user in text.
severity_rules.remediation: Associate error text with `aria-describedby`; surface the error in the accessible name on focus.

severity_rules.pattern: Page lacks a `lang` attribute on `<html>` or has inconsistent navigation order across pages.
severity_rules.description: WCAG 2.1 SC 3.1.1 (A) and 3.2.3 (AA) — the page language must be programmatically determined and navigation must be consistent.
severity_rules.remediation: Set `<html lang="...">`; keep header/nav placement consistent across the site.

severity_rules.pattern: Custom interactive widget (toggle, tab, modal) has no role, name, or state exposed via ARIA or semantic HTML.
severity_rules.description: WCAG 2.1 SC 4.1.2 (A) — name, role, and value of UI components must be programmatically determinable.
severity_rules.remediation: Use a semantic element (`<button>`, `<dialog>`) or apply ARIA role/state with a matching keyboard model.

severity_rules.pattern: Status update (toast, inline confirmation, async result) is rendered without an `aria-live` region or `role="status"`.
severity_rules.description: WCAG 2.1 SC 4.1.3 (AA) — status messages must be programmatically determinable through ARIA live regions.
severity_rules.remediation: Wrap status text in `role="status"` (polite) or `role="alert"` (assertive); avoid stealing focus.
